info.name: Microsoft Exchange Microsoft Graph Calendar API

params.name: $top
params.description: The number of items to return

params.name: $skip
params.description: The number of items to skip

params.name: $select
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to include in the response

items.docs: Get all the user's calendars, or the calendars in the default or other specific calendar group. Returns the calendar collection for the signed-in user.
Create a new calendar in the default calendar group or specified calendar group for the user.

params.name: calendar-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the calendar

params.name: $select
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to include in the response

items.docs: Get the properties and relationships of a calendar object. The calendar can be one for a user or the default calendar of a Microsoft 365 group.

params.name: calendar-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the calendar

items.docs: Update the properties of a calendar object. The calendar can be one for a user or the default calendar of a Microsoft 365 group.

params.name: calendar-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the calendar

items.docs: Delete a calendar other than the user's default calendar.

info.name: Microsoft Exchange List events in calendar

params.name: calendar-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the calendar

params.name: $top
params.description: The number of items to return

params.name: $skip
params.description: The number of items to skip

params.name: $select
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to include in the response

params.name: $filter
params.description: OData filter expression

params.name: $orderby
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to sort by

items.docs: Retrieve a list of events in a specified calendar. The list contains single-instance meetings and series masters.

info.name: Microsoft Exchange Create event in calendar

params.name: calendar-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the calendar

items.docs: Create a new event in the specified calendar.

params.name: $top
params.description: The number of items to return

params.name: $skip
params.description: The number of items to skip

params.name: $select
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to include in the response

params.name: $filter
params.description: OData filter expression

params.name: $orderby
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to sort by

items.docs: Retrieve a list of event objects in the user's mailbox. The list contains single-instance meetings and series masters. Supports OData query parameters to filter, order, and page results.
Create a new event in the user's default calendar or specified calendar. Supports creating single-instance and recurring events, and events with online meeting information.

params.name: event-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the event

params.name: $select
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to include in the response

items.docs: Get the properties and relationships of an event object. Returns the event details including attendees, location, recurrence, and online meeting information.

params.name: event-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the event

items.docs: Update the properties of an event object. When updating the start or end time zones, use the list of supported time zones.

params.name: event-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the event

items.docs: Delete an event from the user's calendar. If the event is a meeting and the user is the organizer, a cancellation message is sent to all attendees.

params.name: event-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the event

items.docs: Accept the specified event in a user calendar. Optionally sends a response to the organizer indicating the user has accepted.

params.name: event-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the event

items.docs: Tentatively accept the specified event in a user calendar.

params.name: event-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the event

items.docs: Decline invitation to the specified event in a user calendar.

params.name: event-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the event

items.docs: Send a cancellation message from the organizer to all the attendees and cancel the specified meeting. Only the organizer can cancel a meeting.

params.name: event-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the event

items.docs: Forward a meeting request to a new recipient. The organizer or attendee forwards the meeting request to a new recipient.

params.name: event-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the event

params.name: startDateTime
params.description: The start date and time of the time range in ISO 8601 format

params.name: endDateTime
params.description: The end date and time of the time range in ISO 8601 format

params.name: $top
params.description: The number of items to return

params.name: $skip
params.description: The number of items to skip

items.docs: Get the occurrences of a recurring series within a specified time range. If the event is a seriesMaster type, this returns the occurrences and exceptions of that series.

params.name: event-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the event

params.name: $select
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to include in the response

items.docs: Retrieve a list of attachment objects attached to an event.

params.name: event-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the event

items.docs: Add a file, item, or reference attachment to an event. This operation limits the size of the attachment to 3 MB.

params.name: startDateTime
params.description: The start date and time of the time range in ISO 8601 format

params.name: endDateTime
params.description: The end date and time of the time range in ISO 8601 format

params.name: $top
params.description: The number of items to return

params.name: $skip
params.description: The number of items to skip

params.name: $select
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to include in the response

params.name: $filter
params.description: OData filter expression

items.docs: Get the occurrences, exceptions, and single instances of events in a calendar view defined by a time range, from the user's primary calendar. Recurring events are expanded into their occurrences within the time range.
Get the free/busy availability information for a collection of users, distribution lists, or resources for a specified time period.
Suggest meeting times and locations based on organizer and attendee availability, and time or location constraints specified as parameters.
